answers: Mindfulness is the practice of being fully present in the moment, aware of your thoughts and feelings without judging them. It reduces stress and improves focus.
Mindfulness practice korte: slowly breathe, tomar surroundings notice koro, khabar khaoar somoy taste feel koro, ar thoughts ke judge na kore observe koro. Present moment e theko.

Mindfulness and meditation are related but different. Meditation is a formal practice, while mindfulness can be practiced anytime — while eating, walking, or even washing dishes. Both train your attention.
Start with 5 minutes daily: sit quietly, focus on your breath, and when your mind wanders, gently bring it back. You can also try mindful eating, walking, or body scan exercises throughout the day.
